title: RSI Mean-Reversion (Connors RSI-2)
idea: Crowds overreact to short-term moves and then revert (De Bondt & Thaler 1985; Jegadeesh 1990). A 2-day RSI extreme is a proxy for that overreaction.
signal_rule: Enter long when RSI(2) < 10; exit when RSI(2) > 90; hold in between.

trend_gbm.regime: Geometric Brownian Motion, +15%/yr drift — a persistent uptrend.
trend_gbm.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe -0.17 (CAGR -3.3%) — a adverse regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

mean_rev_ou.regime: Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process oscillating around 100 — pure mean reversion.
mean_rev_ou.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.14 (CAGR +1.1%) — a marginal regime for this edge; the result is too weak to confirm or reject the thesis. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

regime_switch.regime: 200-bar blocks alternating trend and mean reversion — punishes regime overfitting.
regime_switch.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.99 (CAGR +11.3%) — a favourable regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 100% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

fat_tail.regime: Zero-drift Student-t(3) returns — extreme daily moves ~2–4× more often than normal.
fat_tail.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.73 (CAGR +8.6%) — a favourable regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.
